severity.impact: Engine in derate — machine unable to operate at full capacity. DEF system fault confirmed.

diagnosis.narrative: Primary fault is a low DEF tank level sensor malfunction triggering fault code 3714, which has induced engine derate (3712). DEF level reads 8% — below the 10% derate threshold. Two historical cases with identical fault codes were resolved by sensor replacement with 100% success rate. Recommend replacing DEF Tank Level Sensor (CUM-4309047).

warnings: DPF/SCR components may be extremely hot — allow 60 min cool-down before service
DEF fluid is corrosive — wear gloves and eye protection
DEF level critically low (8%) — refill required before returning to operation

escalation_reason.label: Parts / Warranty & Billing
escalation_reason.reason: DEF Tank Level Sensor requires replacement. Warranty status unclear — need authorisation to proceed before pulling the part.

escalation.narrative: Priority 1 escalation on X15 CM2450 (S/N X15-CM2450-0001) at Summit Construction Site 4B, Denver. Engine is in derate due to DEF sensor malfunction (3714). Tech-014 has completed RCA — sensor replacement confirmed as fix. Warranty authorisation required before proceeding. Parts cost $455. SLA expires in 0.5 hours.

steps.label: Check DEF tank level physically
steps.result: Tank reads 8% on gauge — confirmed low. Sensor reading inconsistent with physical check.

steps.label: Inspect DEF quality and contamination
steps.result: DEF sample clear, no contamination detected.

steps.label: Test DEF level sensor resistance
steps.result: Sensor resistance out of spec — reads 4.2kΩ vs expected 1.8–2.2kΩ. Sensor faulty.

steps.label: Check wiring harness to sensor
steps.result: Continuity confirmed on all pins. No wiring fault.

steps.label: Check DEF pump and dosing injector
steps.result: Pump operating correctly. No injector fault codes active.

severity.impact: DEF system derate active. Machine running at reduced power. Operator unable to complete grading work.

diagnosis.narrative: Fault code 3714 indicates DEF Tank Level Low. Physical inspection required to confirm whether the tank is actually low or the sensor is faulty. Given equipment hours (11,800h) a sensor replacement is likely. DEF level reading 14% — borderline threshold.

warnings: DPF/SCR components may be extremely hot — allow 60 min cool-down before service
DEF fluid is corrosive — wear gloves and eye protection

escalation_reason.label: Unsafe to Proceed — Safety Stop
escalation_reason.reason: Machine is parked on a live highway shoulder. Traffic passing at speed. Unable to safely perform inspection without road closure or site supervisor present to manage traffic control.

escalation.narrative: Safety stop raised on X15 CM2350 at Valley Road Contractors, Route 80, Cheyenne. Machine parked on active highway shoulder — tech TECH-002 unable to proceed without traffic control. Supervisor David Reyes notified. Site must establish safe work zone before service can continue.

severity.impact: Active derate — machine down. Suspected wrong fluid (diesel) added to DEF tank. SCR system at risk of contamination damage.

diagnosis.narrative: Fault codes 3714, 3241, and 3712 indicate DEF quality failure alongside low level and engine derate. Operator confirmed refilling DEF but warning persisted — strongly suggests wrong fluid contamination. SCR catalyst may be damaged. Immediate flush of DEF tank and quality check required. If SCR contaminated, catalyst replacement will be necessary.

warnings: Do not restart engine — SCR catalyst may be contaminated
DEF tank contents unknown — treat as hazardous until tested
DPF components hot — allow cool-down before inspection

steps.label: Sample DEF tank fluid
steps.result: Fluid sample tested — diesel contamination confirmed at approximately 30% mix. DEF tank must be fully drained and flushed.

steps.label: Check SCR catalyst condition
steps.result: SCR inlet temps elevated. Catalyst shows early signs of poisoning. Recommend replacement to prevent further damage.

steps.label: Inspect DEF pump and lines
steps.result: Pump and lines also contaminated. Full system flush required.

steps.label: Check engine for secondary faults
steps.result: No additional engine faults. Derate solely caused by DEF/SCR fault chain.

approval_request.test_results: DEF tank flushed and refilled with correct fluid. SCR catalyst replaced. All fault codes cleared. Engine returned to full power — no derate active. Post-repair road test completed, no recurrence.
approval_request.tech_notes: Operator confirmed they added diesel by mistake thinking it was the DEF fill point. Both caps now labelled clearly on machine. Recommend customer staff training on fluid identification.
